objective: Apply a fork-local runtime hotfix to restore RunPod nightly build reliability by updating the default pinned vLLM nightly to a currently available exact build.
scope: Update Dockerfile ARG VLLM_NIGHTLY_VERSION only and refresh aiwf.request.json governance fields; keep nightly retry+fallback logic and stable install branch unchanged; no upstream interactions.
acceptance_criteria: Dockerfile defaults VLLM_NIGHTLY_VERSION to 0.16.1rc1.dev184+gae88468bc (an available exact nightly build).
Nightly install branch keeps existing pinned-attempt retry and latest-nightly fallback behavior unchanged.
VLLM_NIGHTLY=true continues to enforce nightly-channel installs only; stable path remains gated behind VLLM_NIGHTLY=false.
No unrelated files or upstream remotes are modified.
rollback_plan: Fork-local rollback is a single commit that restores Dockerfile ARG VLLM_NIGHTLY_VERSION to 0.16.1rc1.dev141+g792a74b97 and reverts this request record; retry+fallback logic stays untouched in both forward and rollback paths.
deploy_plan: After local validation, run one fork-only RunPod build using default nightly settings, verify vLLM install/import at container startup, and promote only after successful smoke verification.
threat_model: Primary risk is pinned nightly churn (wheel pruning) causing future install drift; changing only the default pinned value to a known-available exact build lowers immediate failure risk while preserving existing retry/fallback and stable rollback branch behavior.
